text:
Jappon (or simply Japan in Viz's version) is an island country in the world.

Overview

Being the home country of sushi, ninjas, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 2, Chapter 11 haiku, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 68 and samurai, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 399 it is the fictional equivalent of real-life Japan. Hanzo, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 5, Chapter 38 Basho, and Yokotani come from this country.

Trivia

Every "Japponese" character in the series is named after a famous person in Japanese history.
Jappon is a portmanteau of the words "Japan" and "Nippon," "Nippon" being the formal reading of the kanji "日本" meaning Japan.
Jappon's real-life counterpart, Japan, is the only landmark on the real-world map that has not been rotated or flipped to produce its counterpart on Hunter × Hunter's world map.
Although the country's name is written with Roman alphabet characters in its original form in the manga, the Viz's version still transliterates the name back to its inspiration and edits "Japan" over the name "Jappon". 
The people of Jappon phrase their answers negative questions like "You haven't done it?" with "Yes, I have not." instead of "No, I have not.", focusing on the questioner's perspective for some reason, which is suggested to be because of an old hierarchy mindset or the philosophy of selfless hospitality. In some dialects, the pronoun "self" when used self-referentially is used in a second-person context, something that Yokotani's companions note him doing. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 407
